items.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
items.body: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

items.title: Trump plans generic drug tariffs from 2028 with two-year delay testing U.S. onshoring push
items.body: The latest tariff salvo underscored Trump's goal of reshoring low-cost drug production, but displacing established suppliers like India won't be easy.

items.title: Import prices post surprise gain as costs of goods from China hit highest since 2008
items.body: Import prices were up 0.3% for the month, as a drop in energy was more than offset by increases elsewhere.

items.title: World Cup gave bars and restaurants a needed boost as consumers flash warning signs, Fed says
items.body: The latest edition of the Fed's Beige Book indicated that the soccer tournament wasn't necessarily translating to broader economic growth.

items.title: Wholesale prices unexpectedly declined 0.3% in June on big drop in gasoline
items.body: As with consumer prices, the index benefited from easing energy costs, particularly as oil fell due to the brief pause in tensions between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: Renewed Hormuz hostilities drive ECB rates rethink amid ‘extremely volatile’ outlook
items.body: Several consecutive days of strikes exchanged between the U.S. and Iran have cast uncertainty on the European Central Bank's interest rate decision next week.

items.title: China posts slowest quarterly growth since 2022 as investment slumps, fanning stimulus calls
items.body: That second-quarter growth came below Beijing's full-year growth target range of 4.5% to 5%, the least ambitious goal in decades.

items.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
items.body: The consumer price index in June was expected to increase 3.8% from a year ago.
